creator: matthias@d2ux.net
text:
To increase the benefit of having Tryton in the ports collection I began to port the Tryton modules (http://doc.tryton.org/2.4/modules/index.html). The modules provide basic data models and behavior extending the Tryton core to be used a general ledger / invoicing / stock management and bookkeeping application. I plan to port GNU Health too (http://health.gnu.org/).

During the effort to prototypical port the trytond_product module I found out that it would be useful to rename trytond to py-trytond to ensure the PYTHON_PKGNAMEPREFIX can be used for dependency resolution in the modules Makefiles.

Therefore I would like to discuss if it is reasonable to rename the trytond port to py-trytond (with the related changes to the Makefile).

The proposed files for the resulting py-trytond port are:
